  .seg-meta { font-size: 10.5px; color: #6f6a5c; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; margin-left: auto; }

  .topbar { height: 54px; flex: none; border-bottom: 1px solid #221f18; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 16px; padding: 0 22px; background: #141209; }
  .topbar .title { font-size: 15px; font-weight: 700; color: #f5f1e8; letter-spacing: -0.01em; }
  .topbar .sub { font-size: 10.5px; color: #7d7565; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; margin-top: 1px; }
  .search { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; height: 34px; padding: 0 12px; background: #1b1810; border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 8px; width: 280px; flex: none; margin-left: auto; }
  .search input { flex: 1; background: transparent; border: none; outline: none; color: #e9e4d8; font-size: 13px; }
  .search svg { flex: none; }

  .filterbar { flex: none; overflow: hidden; transition: height .2s; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 0 22px; background: #121009; }
  .filterbar.active { height: 52px; border-bottom: 1px solid #1c1912; }
  .filterbar.hidden { height: 0; padding-top: 0; padding-bottom: 0; }
  .filterbar select { height: 30px; background: #1b1810; color: #cfc8b8; border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 7px; font-size: 12px; padding: 0 8px; outline: none; }
  .filter-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; padding: 6px 11px; border-radius: 7px; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid #2a261d; background: #1b1810; color: #8a8273; transition: border-color .15s; }
  .filter-chip.on { border-color: #3a2f1a; background: #241d12; color: #f6a429; }
  .filter-meta { font-size: 11.5px; color: #9a927f; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; margin-left: auto; }

  /* view stack */
  .viewstack { flex: 1; position: relative; min-height: 0; min-width: 0; }
  .view { position: absolute; inset: 0; display: none; }
  .view.active { display: block; }
  .view.scroll { overflow-y: auto; }

  /* overview */
  .overview { padding: 22px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; max-width: 1280px; }
  .kpi-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 14px; }
  .kpi { background: #161309; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 12px; padding: 16px; }
  .kpi.accent { background: linear-gradient(160deg, #1f1608, #161309); border-color: rgba(246,164,41,0.3); }
  .kpi .label { font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #7d7565; font-weight: 700; }
  .kpi.accent .label { color: #c79a52; }
  .kpi .value { font-size: 30px; font-weight: 800; color: #f0ebdf; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; margin-top: 7px; }
  .kpi.accent .value { color: #f6a429; }
  .kpi .note { font-size: 11px; color: #7d7565; margin-top: 2px; }
  .kpi .note.up { color: #5fd08a; }
  .kpi .note.alert { color: #ff8a7e; }
  .kpi-inline { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 9px; margin-top: 7px; }

  .panel { background: #141209; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 12px; }
  .panel .head { padding: 13px 14px 8px; font-size: 11px; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: #7d7565; font-weight: 700; }
  .panel-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1.3fr 1fr; gap: 14px; }

  .funnel { display: flex; align-items: stretch; gap: 10px; padding: 18px 20px; background: #141209; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 12px; }
  .funnel-step { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; }
  .funnel-bar { width: 100%; border-radius: 10px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 800; color: #f5f1e8; }
  .funnel-bar.qual { background: linear-gradient(160deg, #f6a429, #c2641a); color: #1a1000; }
  .funnel-step .name { font-size: 11px; color: #a39c8c; font-weight: 600; margin-top: 8px; }
  .funnel-step .note { font-size: 10px; color: #6f6a5c; margin-top: 1px; }

  /* tables */
  .table-shell { margin: 6px 22px 22px; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 12px; background: #141209; overflow: hidden; }
  .table-scroll { overflow-x: auto; }
  .table-inner { min-width: 1080px; }
  .table-head { display: grid; gap: 0; padding: 11px 14px; background: #1a1710; border-bottom: 1px solid #221f18; font-size: 9.5px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #7d7565; font-weight: 700; }
  .table-head .col { white-space: nowrap; }
  .table-head .col.sort { cursor: pointer; transition: color .15s; }
  .table-head .col.sort:hover { color: #c79a52; }
  .table-head .col.sort.active { color: #c79a52; }
  .table-row { display: grid; gap: 0; padding: 12px 14px; border-bottom: 1px solid #1c1912; align-items: center; }
  .cell { min-width: 0; padding-right: 10px; }
  .cell.ellipsis { white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }
  .cell .name { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #f0ebdf; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }
  .cell .sub { font-size: 10.5px; color: #7d7565; }
  .cell .city { font-size: 12px; color: #cfc8b8; }
  .cell .state { font-size: 10px; color: #7d7565; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }
  .cell.mono { font-size: 12.5px; color: #cfc8b8; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }
  .cell.muted { font-size: 11.5px; color: #a39c8c; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }
  .cell.italic { color: #6f6a5c; font-style: italic; }

  .forge-cell { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding-right: 12px; }
  .forge-bar { flex: 1; height: 6px; background: #241f16; border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; }
  .forge-bar > div { height: 100%; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #c2641a, #f6a429); border-radius: 4px; }
  .forge-num { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; color: #f6a429; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }

  /* mandate pill */
  .mandate { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; border-radius: 5px; padding: 2px 7px; white-space: nowrap; }
  .mandate.upcoming { color: #f6a429; background: rgba(246,164,41,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(246,164,41,0.3); }
  .mandate.inforce { color: #5fd08a; background: rgba(95,208,138,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(95,208,138,0.3); }
  .mandate.enforcement { color: #ff6b5e; background: rgba(255,107,94,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(255,107,94,0.3); }
  .mandate.none { color: #9a927f; background: rgba(154,146,127,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(154,146,127,0.25); }

  /* tier badge */
  .tier-badge { display: inline-flex; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; border-radius: 5px; padding: 2px 7px; white-space: nowrap; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }
  .tier-A { color: #f6a429; background: rgba(246,164,41,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(246,164,41,0.3); }
  .tier-B { color: #5bb8d4; background: rgba(91,184,212,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(91,184,212,0.3); }
  .tier-C { color: #5fd08a; background: rgba(95,208,138,0.12); border: 1px solid rgba(95,208,138,0.3); }
  .tier-X { color: #6f6a5c; background: rgba(111,106,92,0.1); border: 1px solid rgba(111,106,92,0.25); }

  /* map */
  .mapbox { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: radial-gradient(ellipse 80% 70% at 50% 40%, #16140d 0%, #100f0c 72%); }
  .mapbox svg { position: absolute; inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; display: block; }
  .map-tip { position: absolute; pointer-events: none; opacity: 0; transform: translate(-50%, -130%); background: #211e15; border: 1px solid #34301f; border-radius: 7px; padding: 7px 10px; font-size: 11px; color: #f5f1e8; white-space: nowrap; z-index: 30; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); transition: opacity .1s; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }
  .map-card { position: absolute; background: rgba(20,18,11,0.82); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 10px; z-index: 20; }
  .map-card.tl { left: 18px; top: 16px; padding: 8px 12px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; font-size: 11px; border-radius: 9px; }
  .map-card.tl .num { color: #f6a429; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; }
  .map-card.bl { left: 18px; bottom: 18px; padding: 13px 15px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; }
  .map-card.bl .hdr { font-size: 9.5px; letter-spacing: 0.16em; color: #7d7565; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 1px; }
  .map-card.bl .row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; font-size: 11.5px; color: #cfc8b8; }
  .map-card.bl .sep { height: 1px; background: #2a261d; margin: 2px 0; }
  .map-card.bl .dot1 { width: 13px; height: 13px; border-radius: 50%; border: 2px solid #fff; background: #f6a429; box-shadow: 0 0 9px rgba(246,164,41,0.8); flex: none; }
  .map-card.bl .dot2 { width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%; background: #ff5c4d; box-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(255,92,77,0.7); flex: none; }
  .map-card.bl .dot3 { width: 11px; height: 11px; border-radius: 50%; background: #ffcf6b; flex: none; }
  .map-card.bl .dot4 { width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%; background: #e0883a; flex: none; }
  .map-card.bl .dot5 { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: #9a7c52; flex: none; }
  .map-zoom { right: 18px; bottom: 18px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 10px; }
  .map-zoom-btn { width: 38px; height: 38px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: pointer; color: #cfc8b8; border-bottom: 1px solid #2a261d; transition: background .15s; }
  .map-zoom-btn:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
  .map-zoom-btn:hover { background: #211e15; }

  /* detail drawer */
  .drawer-mask { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.4); z-index: 40; display: none; }
  .drawer-mask.open { display: block; }
  .drawer { position: fixed; right: 0; top: 0; bottom: 0; width: 460px; background: #141209; border-left: 1px solid #2a261d; box-shadow: -10px 0 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); z-index: 41; transform: translateX(100%); transition: transform .32s cubic-bezier(0.2,0.7,0.2,1); overflow-y: auto; }
  .drawer.open { transform: translateX(0); }
  .drawer-head { padding: 18px 20px 14px; border-bottom: 1px solid #221f18; display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 12px; }
  .drawer-close { width: 28px; height: 28px; border-radius: 7px; background: #1b1810; border: 1px solid #2a261d; color: #8a8273; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: pointer; flex: none; }
  .drawer h2 { font-size: 17px; font-weight: 700; color: #f5f1e8; line-height: 1.25; }
  .drawer .sub { font-size: 11.5px; color: #7d7565; margin-top: 3px; }
  .drawer .body { padding: 16px 20px 24px; }
  .drawer .section { margin-bottom: 18px; }
  .drawer .section-head { font-size: 9.5px; letter-spacing: 0.14em; color: #7d7565; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; }
  .drawer .chips { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; }
  .drawer .chip { font-size: 11px; padding: 6px 10px; border-radius: 7px; background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #221f18; color: #cfc8b8; }
  .drawer .chip.on { color: #9fe0ba; }
  .drawer .stat-row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 12px; padding: 5px 0; color: #cfc8b8; }
  .drawer .stat-row span:first-child { color: #8a8273; }
  .drawer .forge-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-bottom: 8px; }
  .drawer .forge-row .lbl { width: 90px; font-size: 11px; color: #a39c8c; }
  .drawer .forge-row .bar { flex: 1; height: 6px; background: #241f16; border-radius: 4px; overflow: hidden; }
  .drawer .forge-row .bar > div { height: 100%; background: linear-gradient(90deg, #c2641a, #f6a429); border-radius: 4px; }
  .drawer .forge-row .v { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: #f6a429; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; width: 30px; text-align: right; }
  .drawer .empty { font-size: 12px; color: #6f6a5c; font-style: italic; background: #161309; border: 1px dashed #2a261d; border-radius: 9px; padding: 11px 13px; }
  .drawer .qso-cta { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 10px 14px; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f6a429, #c2641a); border-radius: 9px; color: #1a1000; font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 700; cursor: pointer; border: none; }

  .empty-state { padding: 40px; text-align: center; color: #6f6a5c; font-size: 13px; }
  .stub-pane { padding: 40px 22px; max-width: 600px; }
  .stub-pane h3 { font-size: 16px; color: #f5f1e8; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 8px; }
  .stub-pane p { font-size: 13px; color: #a39c8c; line-height: 1.6; }

  /* ===== CODE MODE ===== */
  .code-shell { display: flex; height: 100%; min-height: 0; background: #100f0c; }
  .code-sidebar { width: 280px; flex: none; background: #161309; border-right: 1px solid #221f18; overflow-y: auto; padding: 8px 4px; }
  .code-main { flex: 1; display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-width: 0; }
  .code-header { flex: none; height: 42px; border-bottom: 1px solid #221f18; padding: 0 22px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 11px; background: #141209; }
  .code-path { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #cfc8b8; }
  .code-path .dim { color: #7d7565; }
  .code-meta { margin-left: auto; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 10.5px; color: #6f6a5c; }
  .code-content { flex: 1; overflow: auto; padding: 22px 28px; background: #100f0c; }
  .code-empty { color: #6f6a5c; font-size: 13px; text-align: center; padding: 60px 20px; }
  .code-md { max-width: 820px; color: #cfc8b8; font-size: 13.5px; line-height: 1.65; }
  .code-md h1 { font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; color: #f5f1e8; margin: 16px 0 8px; }
  .code-md h2 { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; color: #f0ebdf; margin: 18px 0 6px; padding-top: 8px; border-top: 1px solid #221f18; }
  .code-md h3 { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 700; color: #e3ddcf; margin: 14px 0 4px; }
  .code-md p { margin: 8px 0; }
  .code-md ul, .code-md ol { margin: 8px 0 8px 22px; }
  .code-md li { margin: 4px 0; }
  .code-md strong { color: #f0ebdf; font-weight: 700; }
  .code-md em { color: #d8d2c4; }
  .code-md code { background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 3px; padding: 1px 6px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; color: #9fe0ba; }
  .code-md pre { background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 8px; padding: 14px 18px; overflow-x: auto; margin: 12px 0; }
  .code-md pre code { background: none; border: none; padding: 0; color: #cfc8b8; font-size: 12.5px; line-height: 1.55; }
  .code-md a { color: #5bb8d4; text-decoration: underline; }
  .code-md table { border-collapse: collapse; margin: 12px 0; font-size: 12.5px; max-width: 100%; }
  .code-md th, .code-md td { border: 1px solid #221f18; padding: 6px 10px; text-align: left; }
  .code-md th { background: #19160e; color: #e3ddcf; font-weight: 700; font-size: 11.5px; letter-spacing: 0.04em; }
  .code-md hr { border: none; border-top: 1px solid #221f18; margin: 18px 0; }
  .code-md blockquote { border-left: 3px solid #f6a429; padding: 4px 0 4px 14px; margin: 12px 0; color: #b4ac9b; font-style: italic; }
  .code-pre { background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px 20px; max-width: 1100px; overflow-x: auto; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.55; color: #cfc8b8; white-space: pre; tab-size: 4; }
  .code-csv { max-width: 100%; overflow-x: auto; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11.5px; }
  .code-csv table { border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; }
  .code-csv th, .code-csv td { border: 1px solid #221f18; padding: 6px 10px; text-align: left; white-space: nowrap; }
  .code-csv th { background: #19160e; color: #c79a52; font-weight: 700; position: sticky; top: 0; }
  .code-csv td { color: #cfc8b8; }
  .code-csv .truncated-note { color: #7d7565; font-style: italic; padding: 8px 0; font-size: 11px; font-family: 'Hanken Grotesk', sans-serif; }

  /* file tree */
  .ft-dir { padding: 2px 0; }
  .ft-dir-label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; padding: 4px 10px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11.5px; font-weight: 700; color: #7d7565; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; }
  .ft-dir-label:hover { color: #cfc8b8; }
  .ft-dir.collapsed > .ft-children { display: none; }
  .ft-dir-arrow { font-size: 9px; width: 10px; display: inline-block; }
  .ft-children { padding-left: 14px; border-left: 1px solid #1c1912; margin-left: 8px; }
  .ft-file { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 10px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 11.5px; color: #a39c8c; cursor: pointer; border-radius: 4px; }
  .ft-file:hover { color: #f0ebdf; background: #1d1a13; }
  .ft-file.active { color: #f6a429; background: #221d12; }
  .ft-file-icon { font-size: 10px; width: 12px; text-align: center; opacity: 0.7; }
  .ft-file-size { margin-left: auto; font-size: 9.5px; color: #5f5a4e; }

  /* ===== CHAT MODE ===== */
  .chat-shell { display: flex; flex-direction: column; height: 100%; min-height: 0; max-width: 800px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 24px 28px 0; }
  .chat-greeting { font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; color: #f5f1e8; letter-spacing: -0.02em; margin-bottom: 4px; }
  .chat-tagline { font-size: 13px; color: #8a8273; line-height: 1.55; margin-bottom: 22px; max-width: 580px; }
  .chat-tagline strong { color: #c79a52; font-weight: 600; }
  .chat-suggestions { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 10px; margin-bottom: 18px; max-width: 700px; }
  .chat-suggestion { display: flex; gap: 11px; align-items: flex-start; padding: 13px 14px; background: #161309; border: 1px solid #221f18; border-radius: 11px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color .15s, background .15s; }
  .chat-suggestion:hover { border-color: #3a2f1a; background: #1a160d; }
  .chat-suggestion .dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; margin-top: 5px; flex: none; }
  .chat-suggestion .text { flex: 1; font-size: 13px; color: #e3ddcf; line-height: 1.4; }
  .chat-suggestion .text .sub { color: #7d7565; font-size: 11px; margin-top: 2px; }

  .chat-conv { flex: 1; overflow-y: auto; padding: 4px 0 16px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 14px; min-height: 0; }
  .chat-msg { display: flex; max-width: 100%; }
  .chat-msg.user { justify-content: flex-end; }
  .chat-msg.user .bubble { background: #221d12; border: 1px solid #2c2718; max-width: 75%; }
  .chat-msg.assistant .bubble { background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #221f18; max-width: 88%; }
  .chat-msg.task .bubble { background: #161309; border: 1px dashed #2a261d; max-width: 88%; }
  .bubble { padding: 12px 14px; border-radius: 11px; color: #e3ddcf; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.55; }
  .bubble strong { color: #f0ebdf; font-weight: 700; }
  .bubble a { color: #5bb8d4; text-decoration: underline; }
  .bubble .cards { display: grid; gap: 8px; margin-top: 10px; }
  .bubble .card { display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: flex-start; padding: 10px 12px; background: #100f0c; border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 9px; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color .15s; }
  .bubble .card:hover { border-color: #3a2f1a; background: #141209; }
  .bubble .card .badge { font-size: 8.5px; font-weight: 800; letter-spacing: 0.05em; padding: 3px 6px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; flex: none; margin-top: 1px; }
  .bubble .card .card-text { flex: 1; }
  .bubble .card .card-title { font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 600; color: #f0ebdf; }
  .bubble .card .card-sub { font-size: 11px; color: #7d7565; margin-top: 2px; }
  .bubble .task-line { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 9px; }
  .bubble .task-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 50%; flex: none; }

  .chat-input-bar { flex: none; display: flex; gap: 9px; padding: 12px 0 22px; align-items: center; border-top: 1px solid #221f18; margin-top: 6px; }
  .chat-input { flex: 1; height: 40px; padding: 0 14px; background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 10px; color: #e9e4d8; font-size: 13px; outline: none; }
  .chat-input:focus { border-color: #3a2f1a; }
  .chat-send { height: 40px; padding: 0 18px; background: #f6a429; border: none; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 12.5px; font-weight: 700; color: #1a1000; cursor: pointer; }
  .chat-send:hover { background: #ffb944; }
  .chat-spawn { height: 40px; padding: 0 13px; background: #19160e; border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 10px; color: #cfc8b8; font-size: 12px; cursor: pointer; }
  .chat-spawn:hover { border-color: #3a2f1a; color: #f6a429; }

  .spawn-modal-mask { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.45); z-index: 50; display: none; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }
  .spawn-modal-mask.open { display: flex; }
  .spawn-modal { width: 460px; max-height: 80vh; overflow-y: auto; background: #141209; border: 1px solid #2a261d; border-radius: 14px; padding: 20px 22px; }
  .spawn-modal h3 { font-size: 14px; color: #f5f1e8; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 12px; }
  .spawn-modal .agent { display: flex; gap: 11px; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 9px; cursor: pointer; border: 1px solid transparent; }
  .spawn-modal .agent:hover { background: #19160e; border-color: #221f18; }
  .spawn-modal .agent .dot { width: 9px; height: 9px; border-radius: 50%; margin-top: 4px; flex: none; }
  .spawn-modal .agent .name { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; color: #f0ebdf; }
  .spawn-modal .agent .desc { font-size: 11px; color: #7d7565; margin-top: 1px; }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<div class="app">

          <svg width="13" height="13" viewBox="0 0 14 14" fill="none"><path d="M5 3.8 2.3 7 5 10.2M9 3.8 11.7 7 9 10.2" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"/></svg>

    </div>

    <div class="topbar">
      <div style="min-width:0">

        <div class="filter-chip on" data-tier="2">Medium</div>

        <option value="All">Mandate: all</option>
        <option value="Upcoming">Upcoming</option>
        <option value="In force">In force</option>
        <option value="Enforcement">Enforcement</option>
      </select>
      <select id="f-standalone">
        <option value="All">Standalone: all</option>
        <option value="Primary">Primary only</option>
        <option value="Deprioritized">Deprioritized only</option>
        <option value="Excluded">Excluded only</option>
      </select>

    </div>

    <div class="viewstack" id="viewstack">
      <!-- views injected by JS -->
      <div class="view scroll" data-view="overview"><div class="overview" id="view-overview"></div></div>

        <div class="chat-shell">

            <h2 class="chat-greeting" id="chat-greeting">Good morning, Ryan</h2>
            <p class="chat-tagline">
              Canned-but-clever interface to the engine. Pattern-matches your input
              and surfaces real artifacts. For live LLM-powered agents,
              <strong>spawn one</strong> below — the heavy work runs <em>locally</em> via Python
              skills (Haiku 4.5 + Apify) and outputs commit to the repo.
            </p>
          </header>
          <div class="chat-suggestions" id="chat-suggestions"></div>
          <div class="chat-conv" id="chat-conv"></div>
          <div class="chat-input-bar">
            <input class="chat-input" id="chat-input" placeholder="Ask about a QSO, a mandate, an OSHA incident, FORGE …">
            <button class="chat-send" id="chat-send">Send</button>
            <button class="chat-spawn" id="chat-spawn">+ Spawn agent</button>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>
      <div class="view" data-view="code">
        <div class="code-shell">
          <aside class="code-sidebar">
            <div class="code-tree" id="code-tree"><div class="code-empty" style="padding:20px 8px">Loading…</div></div>
          </aside>
          <main class="code-main">
            <header class="code-header">
              <span class="code-path"><span class="dim">firefly-gtm/</span><span id="code-path-rest"></span></span>
              <span class="code-meta" id="code-meta"></span>
            </header>
            <div class="code-content" id="code-content">
              <div class="code-empty">
                <strong style="display:block;font-size:14px;color:#cfc8b8;margin-bottom:6px">Select a file from the tree.</strong>
                77 files embedded · markdown rendered · code as plain text.<br>
                For the full repo: <a href="https://github.com/ryanmichaels-jpg/firefly-gtm" target="_blank" style="color:#5bb8d4">github.com/ryanmichaels-jpg/firefly-gtm</a>
              </div>
            </div>
          </main>
        </div>
      </div>
      <!-- spawn-agent modal -->

        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </main>

"Legend: Y=enacted/in effect (or signed, phasing) · P=pending/partial · N=none found · * CA retail via SB553 (general-industry) · ~ narrow/penalty-only/not-yet-effective",,,,,,
LEGEND — cell qualifiers,why a plain Y/N isn't the whole story,,,,,
Y,"Enacted and in force (or signed, phasing in)",,,,,
P,Pending — introduced / in committee / passed one chamber; not yet law,,,,,
N,None found,,,,,
Dead,Bill failed at end of session (sine die),,,,,
Vetoed (HBxxxx),Passed the legislature but governor vetoed — not law,,,,,
~  (e.g. Y~),Narrow / penalty-only / not-yet-effective,,,,,
*  (Y*),"Covered indirectly (e.g., CA retail via general-industry SB553)",,,,,
/  (P/Y),"Ambiguous / transitional status (e.g., near-final but unsigned)",,,,,
?  (Y (local?)),"Unconfirmed (e.g., disputed Philadelphia hotel ordinance)",,,,,
(penalty),"No prevention-program/device mandate, but a criminal penalty-enhancement law exists. Counts as N for purchase-driving purposes",,,,,
(no enhancement),"No program mandate AND no penalty law — a true holdout (e.g., Montana)",,,,,
(none enacted),"A bill is pending but nothing is in force yet (e.g., Wyoming)",,,,,
(reporting),"Enacted but only requires incident reporting, not a full program/device",,,,,
(public) / (public §27-b),"Applies to PUBLIC employers only, not private",,,,,
(permissive),Enacted but only 'may' / 'must consider' — no install mandate,,,,,
(permissive/contingent),"Permissive AND funding-contingent (e.g., West Virginia)",,,,,
(consider) / (evaluate),"The statute's actual verb — permissive, not mandatory",,,,,
(mapping) / (mapping only),"A facility-MAPPING mandate, not a panic-alert (Alyssa's) law",,,,,
(program),"A funded state program/initiative, not a statutory mandate",,,,,
(first state)/(every classroom)/(wearable),Scope / notability tags on an enacted mandate,,,,,
(NYC)/(Miami Beach)/(local),Enacted at CITY level only — no statewide law,,,,,
(100+ rooms)/(HB1524),Enacted statewide with a room-count threshold / bill cite,,,,,
General-industry column,"Cross-sector, ALL-employer WPV mandate (e.g., CA SB553). 'N' = no all-employer mandate (state may still have industry-specific ones)",,,,,
For outreach,GOLD = Y enacted + mandatory + dated + applies to your buyer. Score down anything with a softening qualifier (penalty/permissive/public/reporting/mapping/local/~) or P / Dead / Vetoed,,,,,

files.diagrams/generate_source_map.py:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Emit diagrams/source-map.svg — the Firefly TAM pipeline source map.

Style: hand-drawn aesthetic via SVG turbulence filter, all-caps labels above
outlined containers, peach-tinted field rows, violet arrows. Mirrors the
reference screenshots (color-coded containers, ALL-CAPS section labels).

Regenerate after edits:  python3 diagrams/generate_source_map.py
"""
from __future__ import annotations
from pathlib import Path
from dataclasses import dataclass

# ---- layout constants ----
COL_W = 320
ROW_H = 28
ROW_GAP = 4
PAD = 14
LABEL_GAP = 8     # text-label above container
COL_GAP = 100     # gap between source/pipe/mart/down columns
COL_X = {'src': 40, 'pipe': 460, 'mart': 900, 'down': 1320}
CANVAS_W = COL_X['down'] + COL_W + 60
CANVAS_H = 1080

# ---- colors (Excalidraw-ish) ----
COLOR = {
    'src':   '#9B7BC9',   # lavender
    'pipe':  '#E08E45',   # orange
    'mart':  '#69A55C',   # green
    'down':  '#D96B7E',   # pink/coral
    'guard': '#C0392B',   # red
    'row_fill': '#FBEFE0',
    'row_stroke': '#5A3A1A',
    'arrow': '#A23DA8',   # magenta-violet
    'chain_arrow': '#1F1F1F',
    'text': '#1F1F1F',
}

@dataclass
class Container:
    key: str
    x: int
    y: int
    label: str
    rows: list[str]
    outline: str
    @property
    def h(self):
        return PAD + len(self.rows) * (ROW_H + ROW_GAP) - ROW_GAP + PAD
    @property
    def cx(self): return self.x + COL_W // 2
    @property
    def cy(self): return self.y + self.h // 2

# ---- data ----
SOURCES = [
    ('cms-hgi',  'CMS HGI',          ['Hospital list (15 states)', 'ccn · hospital_type · ownership', 'has_ED · mailing address']),
    ('ahrq',     'AHRQ COMPENDIUM',  ['parent_system · health_sys_id', 'facilities_in_system', '98% CCN match']),
    ('pos',      'CMS POS — Q1 2026',['CRTFD_BED_CNT (certified)', 'PSYCH_UNIT_BED_CNT', '→ has_behavioral_unit']),
    ('nppes',    'NPPES',            ['DBA (other_names → name)', 'physical address + zip', 'edm_seed (auth_official)']),
    ('census',   'CENSUS GEOCODER',  ['lat · lng (TIGER, free)', 'batch ≤ 10k · no key', 'retry on 502 · strip PO BOX']),
    ('mandates', 'MANDATES.CSV',     ['Status: Upcoming / In force', 'effective_date (parsed)', 'source_url']),
]
STEPS = [
    ('st1', 'STEP 1 · PULL CMS HGI',    ['→ clean hospital list', 'primary seed (NOT NPPES)']),
    ('st2', 'STEP 2 · DEDUP',           ['key: CCN + name+addr', 'do NOT key on NPI']),
    ('st3', 'STEP 3 · JOIN AHRQ',       ['add parent_system', 'add facilities_in_system']),
    ('st4', 'STEP 4 · JOIN POS',        ['add certified beds', 'add has_behavioral_unit']),
    ('st5', 'STEP 5 · ENRICH NPPES',    ['prefer DBA over legal', 'edm_seed from auth_official']),
    ('st6', 'STEP 6 · GEOCODE',         ['Census batch geocoder', 'retry · address-clean']),
    ('st7', 'STEP 7 · MANDATE-JOIN',    ['status logic (In force / Upcoming)', 'NOT raw countdown']),
    ('st8', 'STEP 8 · TIER',            ['by beds + hospital_type', 'Critical Access → Tier 3']),
]
SRC_TOP_Y, SRC_GAP = 90, 150
PIPE_TOP_Y, PIPE_GAP = 90, 115

src_boxes = {k: Container('src-' + k, COL_X['src'], SRC_TOP_Y + i * SRC_GAP, lbl, rows, COLOR['src'])
             for i, (k, lbl, rows) in enumerate(SOURCES)}
step_boxes = {k: Container(k, COL_X['pipe'], PIPE_TOP_Y + i * PIPE_GAP, lbl, rows, COLOR['pipe'])
              for i, (k, lbl, rows) in enumerate(STEPS)}

mart_box = Container('mart-tam', COL_X['mart'], 260, 'DATA/MART/TAM.CSV  (1 row = facility)', [
    'identity: account_id · ccn · facility_name',
    'footprint: lat,lng · beds · has_ED · has_BH',
    'parent: parent_system · facilities_in_system',
    'mandate: status · effective_date · src_url',
    'edm: edm_seed name · title · phone',
    'meta: facility_tier · confidence · needs_review',
], COLOR['mart'])

forge_box = Container('down-forge', COL_X['down'], 90, 'FORGE SCORE  (pre-call proxies)', [
    'Fit (gate)  · pass / fail',
    'Acute Need  · 0–3',
    'Event       · 0–3 (mandate clock)',
    'Gravity     · 0–3 (sponsor / budget)',
    'TOTAL · TIER · rationale',
    'Resolve / Clarity → confirm on call',
], COLOR['down'])

qso_box = Container('down-qso', COL_X['down'], 380, '5 QSOs  (TIER C · paid enrichment)', [
    'Apify + Prospeo / Apollo',
    'Street View recon (entrances)',
    'is_qso_candidate = TRUE',
    'one-off draft (NOT a sequence)',
], COLOR['down'])

dash_box = Container('down-dash', COL_X['down'], 600, 'DASHBOARD  (static, reads tam.json)', [
    'US map view (mandate states)',
    'Clay-style list view',
    'sortable / filterable by FORGE',
], COLOR['down'])

guard_box = Container('guard', COL_X['down'], 800, 'GUARDRAILS  (never break)', [
    'Never fabricate · unknown = null + needs_review',
    'Never seed NPPES first',
    'Paid (Apify / contacts) only on 5 QSOs',
    'No email sequences — one-offs only',
], COLOR['guard'])

ALL = [*src_boxes.values(), *step_boxes.values(), mart_box, forge_box, qso_box, dash_box, guard_box]

ARROWS_SRC_TO_STEP = [
    ('cms-hgi', 'st1'), ('ahrq', 'st3'), ('pos', 'st4'),
    ('nppes', 'st5'),   ('census', 'st6'), ('mandates', 'st7'),
]
PIPE_CHAIN = [f'st{i}' for i in range(1, 9)]

# ---- SVG emit ----
def esc(s: str) -> str:
    return s.replace('&', '&').replace('<', '<').replace('>', '>')

def container_svg(c: Container) -> str:
    out = []
    out.append(
        f'<text class="lbl" x="{c.x}" y="{c.y - LABEL_GAP}">{esc(c.label)}</text>'
    )
    out.append(
        f'<rect class="container" x="{c.x}" y="{c.y}" width="{COL_W}" height="{c.h}" '
        f'rx="6" ry="6" fill="none" stroke="{c.outline}" stroke-width="2.5"/>'
    )
    for j, r in enumerate(c.rows):
        rx = c.x + 12
        ry = c.y + PAD + j * (ROW_H + ROW_GAP)
        rw = COL_W - 24
        out.append(
            f'<rect class="row" x="{rx}" y="{ry}" width="{rw}" height="{ROW_H}" '
            f'rx="3" ry="3" fill="{COLOR["row_fill"]}" stroke="{COLOR["row_stroke"]}" stroke-width="1"/>'
        )
        out.append(
            f'<text class="row-text" x="{rx + 10}" y="{ry + ROW_H/2 + 5}">{esc(r)}</text>'
        )
    return '\n'.join(out)

def arrow_svg(x1, y1, x2, y2, color, label=None, dashed=False, bend=0.0):
    # cubic bezier curve from (x1,y1) to (x2,y2), bend controls vertical sag
    dx = x2 - x1
    dy = y2 - y1
    c1x = x1 + dx * 0.4
    c1y = y1 + dy * 0.05 + bend
    c2x = x1 + dx * 0.6
    c2y = y2 - dy * 0.05 + bend
    dash = ' stroke-dasharray="6 3"' if dashed else ''
    path = (f'<path d="M {x1} {y1} C {c1x} {c1y}, {c2x} {c2y}, {x2} {y2}" '
            f'fill="none" stroke="{color}" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow-{color.lstrip("#")})"{dash}/>')
    out = [path]
    if label:
        mx, my = (x1 + x2) / 2, (y1 + y2) / 2 + bend - 6
        out.append(f'<text class="edge-label" x="{mx}" y="{my}" fill="{color}">{esc(label)}</text>')
    return '\n'.join(out)

# ---- compose ----
header_y = 38
HEADERS = [
    (COL_X['src'],  'SOURCES'),
    (COL_X['pipe'], 'PIPELINE  (CMS-first · 8 steps)'),
    (COL_X['mart'], 'MART'),
    (COL_X['down'], 'DOWNSTREAM'),
]

# arrow markers (one per color)
arrow_colors = {COLOR['arrow'], COLOR['chain_arrow']}
marker_defs = '\n'.join(
    f'<marker id="arrow-{c.lstrip("#")}" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" '
    f'markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">'
    f'<path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="{c}"/>'
    f'</marker>'
    for c in arrow_colors
)

# build the arrow set
arrow_pieces = []

# sources → pipeline steps (right edge of source → left edge of step)
for s_key, t_key in ARROWS_SRC_TO_STEP:
    s = src_boxes[s_key]
    t = step_boxes[t_key]
    arrow_pieces.append(arrow_svg(
        s.x + COL_W, s.cy, t.x, t.cy,
        COLOR['arrow'], bend=(t.cy - s.cy) * 0.25
    ))

# pipeline chain (bottom of step N → top of step N+1)
for a, b in zip(PIPE_CHAIN[:-1], PIPE_CHAIN[1:]):
    sa = step_boxes[a]
    sb = step_boxes[b]
    arrow_pieces.append(arrow_svg(
        sa.cx, sa.y + sa.h, sb.cx, sb.y,
        COLOR['chain_arrow']
    ))

# step8 → mart
s8 = step_boxes['st8']
arrow_pieces.append(arrow_svg(
    s8.x + COL_W, s8.cy, mart_box.x, mart_box.cy + 60,
    COLOR['arrow'], label='write'
))
# mart → forge
arrow_pieces.append(arrow_svg(
    mart_box.x + COL_W, mart_box.y + 30, forge_box.x, forge_box.cy,
    COLOR['arrow'], label='score'
))
# forge → qso
arrow_pieces.append(arrow_svg(
    forge_box.cx, forge_box.y + forge_box.h, qso_box.cx, qso_box.y,
    COLOR['arrow'], label='top-5'
))
# mart → dashboard
arrow_pieces.append(arrow_svg(
    mart_box.x + COL_W, mart_box.y + mart_box.h - 30, dash_box.x, dash_box.cy,
    COLOR['arrow'], label='render'
))

containers_svg = '\n'.join(container_svg(c) for c in ALL)
arrows_svg = '\n'.join(arrow_pieces)
headers_svg = '\n'.join(
    f'<text class="header" x="{x}" y="{header_y}">{esc(t)}</text>' for x, t in HEADERS
)

svg = f'''<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 {CANVAS_W} {CANVAS_H}"
     width="{CANVAS_W}" height="{CANVAS_H}" font-family="'Patrick Hand','Caveat','Comic Sans MS',cursive">
  <defs>

files.docs/SOURCES.md:
# Data Sources

Every source the Firefly GTM engine reads from, what it gives us, and where it lands. Updated **2026-06-22**.

---

## TL;DR

The engine knows the following about each of the 2,353 facilities in our 15 priority states:

- **Identity**: name, CCN, address, parent system, system size
- **Capacity**: beds, ED flag, behavioral health flag, hospital type
- **Risk profile**: safety-net status, teaching-intensity flags (proxies for WPV exposure)
- **Regulatory pressure**: applicable state WPV mandate, status, effective date
- **Geography**: lat/lng
- **Buyer surface**: NPPES authorized_official title (CEO/CFO/etc.)
- **Score**: FORGE total + tier + rationale per dimension

It does **not** yet know: incumbent vendor, contract expiry, recent incidents (per facility), OSHA citation history, named champion, verified buyer contacts, building footprint. For the 5 hand-picked QSOs, those gaps are filled by hand-research in `documents/qso-briefs/`.

---

## 1. Live-pulled sources (Tier-A pipeline)

These are what `skills/seed-tier-a/run.py` ingests on every run. Output: `data/mart/tam.csv`.

| # | Source | Endpoint | What we pull | What it tells us | Lands in |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **CMS Hospital General Information** | data.cms.gov (CSV download) | Facility ID (CCN), name, address, hospital type, ownership, ED flag | Real hospital? Acute care vs. critical access vs. psychiatric? ED present? Government/non-profit/for-profit? | Seed list. `hospital_type`, `ownership`, `has_ED`. |
| 2 | **AHRQ Compendium of US Health Systems** | ahrq.gov (CSV, cp1252) | health_sys_name, health_sys_id, facilities_in_system, hos_majteach, hos_highdpp, hos_highuc | Multi-facility system? System size? Major teaching hospital? High-DSH (safety-net)? High uncompensated care burden? | `parent_system`, `facilities_in_system`, safety-net flags feed FORGE Acute Need. |
| 3 | **CMS Provider of Services (POS)** Q1 2026 | data.cms.gov (CSV, 30 MB) | CRTFD_BED_CNT, PSYCH_UNIT_BED_CNT | Medicare-certified bed count. Psych/behavioral inpatient unit present? | `beds`, `has_behavioral_unit`. Drives FORGE Fit + tier classification. |
| 4 | **NPPES API** | npiregistry.cms.hhs.gov | DBA name, physical address, NPI, authorized_official (name + title + phone) | Marketing/DBA name. Physical (not billing) location. Legal signing officer. | `facility_name` (DBA override), `address`, `edm_seed_*`. Drives FORGE Gravity. |
| 5 | **Census Geocoder API** | geocoding.geo.census.gov | lat, lng | Map placement. | `lat`, `lng`. Powers dashboard map view. |
| 6 | **mandates.csv** (hand-curated) | LegiScan + state statute URLs | mandate_name, status, effective_date per state | Is there a state WPV statute? In force / Upcoming / Enforcement? When? | `mandate_name`, `mandate_status`, `effective_date`. Entire FORGE Event dimension. |
| 7 | **OSHA Severe Injury Reports** | osha.gov bulk ZIP | Employer, EventDate, Nature, Source, NAICS 622 incidents in last 24mo | Citation-grade WPV / staff-injury evidence at the facility. Lifts FORGE Acute Need from AHRQ proxy to documented federal-OSHA evidence. | `osha_severe_injury_count_24mo`, `osha_first_evidence_url`, `osha_first_evidence_date`, `osha_first_evidence_nature`. Lifts `acute_need` 1/2 → 3 for matched facilities. Federal-OSHA-only (5 of our 15 priority states are state-plan, excluded). |

**Run**: `python3 skills/seed-tier-a/run.py --all` · **Cost**: $0 · **Wall-clock**: ~25 min (NPPES is the bottleneck)

## 2. Reference tables (hand-curated, in repo)

Committed lookup tables the pipeline joins against.

| File | What it is | Used by |
|---|---|---|
| `data/reference/mandates.csv` | 87-row WPV statute database with status + effective dates | Phase 7 of seed-tier-a (mandate-join with state-vs-federal precedence) |
| `data/reference/competitors.csv` | 30+ Firefly competitors classified by surface (full-stack/platform/hardware) and role (replace-layer/integrate-layer/frenemy) | Human reference for Tier-B incumbent-vendor matching |
| `data/reference/coverage-grid.csv` + `coverage-grid-ranked.csv` | State-by-state mandate strength ranking | Justifies 15-state scope decision |
| `data/reference/buying-committee.csv` | Healthcare-vertical buying-committee role map | Methodology for Tier-C 5-QSO research |
| `data/reference/signal-sources.csv` | Catalog of external signal sources with cost/cadence | Operational planning |

## 3. One-time external research (the 5 QSO briefs)

Not in the pipeline — done by hand via WebSearch for `documents/qso-briefs/qso-N-*.md`. Every signal carries a public evidence URL inside the brief.

| Source | What it gave us | Which QSOs |
|---|---|---|
| **Regional news** (KIRO 7, Nurse.org, The Advocate, WBRZ, WAFB, KRIS TV) | Dated incident reports (Harborview Feb 2026, OLOL Jackson Mar 2025, Spohn Nov 2024 hoax) | 1, 4, 5 |
| **AHA case studies** | Documented WPV programs at NYP (EMR flagging), OLOL Health Louisiana | 3, 5 |
| **DHS TVTP grantee stories** | NYP's federal grant work on threat assessment training | 3 |
| **HealthLeaders Media** | RWJBarnabas Health's 5-step WPV framework | 2 |
| **Court records / lawsuit filings** (via news) | Jackson v. OLOL & Inner Parish Security Corp wrongful-death suit | 5 |
| **Belleville Patch** (labor news) | 1199SEIU unionization + NLRB filings at Clara Maass | 2 |
| **Seattle PD blotter** | Harborview incident perpetrator details | 1 |
| **LinkedIn profile view** (no scrape) | Heather Runnels confirmed as OLOL nursing executive | 5 |

## 4. Configured but currently blocked / deferred

| Source | Status | Why blocked | Next step |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Competitor customer pages** (CENTEGIX, RF Tech, CenTrak, Status Solutions, Vocera, Singlewire, Rave, Motorola Solutions) | Phase 1 ran — ~5 real names from 18 raw extractions | Vendor pages mostly use image logos / JS-rendered / Cloudflare gates. Text extraction yield fundamentally low. | Lower priority — yield-limited at the source. |
| **Wayback Machine** (Strongline / Securitas Healthcare) | Tested, 404 | URL guess wrong; needs Availability API call to find real snapshot | Quick fix — ~15 min |
| **Google Custom Search Engine** (site:linkedin.com queries) | Code complete, blocked on Google Cloud 403 | Project-level config issue — not resolvable remotely | User needs to sort Cloud account; then `python3 skills/tier-b-techstack/run.py --phase cse` works |
| **OSHA Severe Injury Reports** | ✅ **NOW LIVE** (see source #7 above) | (resolved — URL was at `osha.gov/sites/default/files/January2015toOctober2025.zip`) | Run `python3 skills/tier-b-osha/run.py --all` to re-match. Auto-refresh requires re-downloading the ZIP when OSHA updates it (periodic). |
| **IRS Form 990 narrative** | Probed via ProPublica, ruled out | ProPublica API exposes financial summary only; current PDFs unavailable; needs AWS S3 e-file XML | ~3-4 hrs infra work; lower priority |
| **News incident feeds at scale** | Considered, not built | Free NewsAPI tier doesn't cover 2,353 facilities | **GDELT is the right answer here — free + unlimited** (see "Next" section below) |
| **USA Spending API** | Probed, ruled out | Federal hospital contracts are mostly lab specimens, not security | Not useful — won't pursue |

## 5. Local-only artifacts (gitignored)

| Path | What's there | Why local-only |
|---|---|---|
| `.env` | API credentials (Google CSE key + ID, others as added) | Per `.gitignore` line `.env*` |
| `data/raw/cms-hgi/`, `data/raw/ahrq-compendium/`, `data/raw/cms-pos/`, `data/raw/nppes/`, `data/raw/census-geocode/`, `data/raw/competitor-pages/` | Downloaded source files + caches | Large + NPPES has PII (per-CCN JSON with auth_official phone) |
| `data/staging/*.csv` | Per-step intermediates (01_cms_hgi.csv → 08_tier.csv) | Regenerable from raw |
| `data/mart/tam.csv` and `data/mart/tam_scored.csv` | Full mart with `edm_seed_phone` and full `edm_seed_name` | PII. `tam_scored_sample.csv` is the PII-redacted committable version. |

## Hard rules these sources respect

From `CLAUDE.md`:
1. **Never fabricate.** Unknown fields are `null` + `needs_review=TRUE`.
2. **Public data only. No PHI.**
3. **Every signal carries a `*_source_url`.**
4. **Paid contact tools reserved for Tier-C** under explicit gate.

## How to regenerate

```bash
# Re-pull + re-score from scratch
python3 skills/seed-tier-a/run.py --all      # ~25 min
python3 skills/forge-score/run.py --all      # ~30 sec
python3 dashboard/build-data.py              # ~5 sec
```

Cached sources (NPPES, Census batches) survive reruns — the only at-scale network operation that re-fires is whatever's missing from cache.

# Compliance Conformance — Firefly × Arizona (AZ)

## Primary mandate

**ARS §36-420.03 (SB1311)** (program+training)

- **Jurisdiction**: Arizona
- **Status**: Enacted
- **Effective / Deadline**: Plan by 7/1/2023
- **Source**: azleg.gov/ars/36/00420-03.htm
- **Requirement (short)**: Hospitals/ED/urgent care >50 employees: written WPV plan, training

## Firefly coverage by requirement theme

The mandate above maps to the following requirement themes; Firefly's coverage per theme:

### Written Program

Not a product feature — but Ember + Lattice deployment generates the audit
trail, incident log, and risk-assessment data that a written WPV program
requires for documentation. Customers typically pair Firefly with their
internal HR / Safety team's policy template.

### Training

Not Firefly's primary scope — typically delivered by training co's
(HSS, Crisis Prevention Institute, AVADE). Firefly integrates with
LMS for incident-driven training triggers.

# Compliance Conformance — Firefly × California (CA)

## Primary mandate

**8 CCR §3342 / Labor Code §6401.8 (SB1299)** (device+program+training)

- **Jurisdiction**: California
- **Status**: Enacted
- **Effective / Deadline**: Log 4/1/2017; plan 4/1/2018
- **Source**: dir.ca.gov/title8/3342.html
- **Requirement (short)**: Hospital WPV plan, hazard assessment, violent-incident log, alarm incl. personal alarm devices

## Firefly coverage by requirement theme

The mandate above maps to the following requirement themes; Firefly's coverage per theme:

### Written Program

Not a product feature — but Ember + Lattice deployment generates the audit
trail, incident log, and risk-assessment data that a written WPV program
requires for documentation. Customers typically pair Firefly with their
internal HR / Safety team's policy template.

### Risk Assessment

Lattice gateway provides building-level coverage maps; Ember dashboard
surfaces incident heat-maps and gap analysis per unit. The data layer
directly feeds annual risk-assessment cycles.

### Panic Alert System

Lattice panic buttons (wearable + fixed), resilient on a private no-IT
RF gateway (5-year battery, no WiFi dependency). Ember routes alerts
to nursing, security, and EMS with location + escalation logic.

### Incident Reporting

Ember incident log — auto-generates structured records per event with
timestamps, location, response actions, and outcome. Exports to OSHA
300 / state reporting formats.

**2019 — Lynne Truxillo (RN, BH unit)**: Patient Anthony Guillory attacked another nurse in OLOL's behavioral health unit at the Mid City campus on April 4, 2019. Truxillo, 56, intervened to pull Guillory off her colleague. Guillory then turned on her, grabbed her by the back of the neck, and struck her head against a desk. She died days later from blood clots ruled by the East Baton Rouge Parish Coroner as directly connected to the assault ([The Advocate](https://www.theadvocate.com/baton_rouge/news/crime_police/article_53f6af60-770c-11e9-b2c6-0ba9db15acf7.html) · [WAFB](https://www.wafb.com/2019/05/06/death-nurse-hands-patient-renewing-focus-workplace-violence/)). The **Lynne Truxillo Act** (R.S. 40:2199.11–.19) was passed in her memory.

**March 19, 2025 — Patricia ("Pattie") Jackson (OLOL employee)**: Shot multiple times by her estranged partner Roland Domino in OLOL's employee parking lot. Domino had been **loitering near her car for hours** before she arrived. He pleaded guilty to second-degree murder in May 2026 ([The Advocate lawsuit coverage](https://www.theadvocate.com/baton_rouge/news/courts/our-lady-of-the-lake-lawsuit-patricia-jackson-killing/article_8451c813-e09d-4dd8-be08-dfdea6e42cab.html) · [WBRZ](https://www.wbrz.com/news/family-suing-hospital-and-security-after-relative-was-murdered-as-she-left-work/) · [WAFB plea coverage](https://www.wafb.com/2026/05/11/man-pleads-guilty-second-degree-murder-hospital-parking-lot-shooting/)).

**Active lawsuit (2025–)**: Jackson's children filed wrongful death against OLOL **and its security contractor, Inner Parish Security Corp**, alleging the contractor "negligently failed in its duties to protect hospital staff by allowing Domino to loiter on hospital premises for hours." The lawsuit is the operational pressure; the incumbent vendor is named.

**Identified incumbent**: Inner Parish Security Corp (named in lawsuit — likely contract guard services, not necessarily the panic/duress/detection layer, but the perimeter-monitoring failure is at the heart of the case).

The Truxillo Act is in force statewide; OLOL is the eponym facility. After Jackson, the moral and legal pressure is at maximum.

## Buying committee

| Role | Name | Source | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| EDM (NPPES seed) | **Chuck Spicer** | NPPES auth_official | CEO, OLOL Regional Medical Center |
| Likely current CNO/champion | **Heather Runnels DNP, RN, NEA-BC** | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/heather-runnels-dnp-rn-nea-bc-332a30198/) | OLOL nursing executive — directly downstream of the Jackson case |
| System sponsor | **Richard Vath** (FMOLHS system CEO) — confirm via fmolhs.org | system-wide post-Jackson decision-maker |  |
| Likely champion | **Director of Security & Safety, OLOL** | confirm via IAHSS LA chapter | role under acute scrutiny post-Jackson lawsuit |
| Influencer | **Lynne Truxillo's surviving family / Truxillo Foundation if exists** | mandate eponym connection |  |
| Incumbent | **Inner Parish Security Corp** | named in Jackson lawsuit | RFP-defense status: contract may be revisited post-litigation |

## One-off draft

**To:** Chuck Spicer, CEO, Our Lady of the Lake Regional Medical Center
**CC:** Heather Runnels (Nursing Executive); Director of Security & Safety, OLOL
**Subject:** The Truxillo Act + Pattie Jackson + Inner Parish — what's next?

Chuck —

The Lynne Truxillo Act is the only state WPV statute I know of named after the eponym hospital's own employee. After Pattie Jackson in March, the legal and moral pressure inside FMOL has to be off the chart — and the Inner Parish lawsuit means perimeter monitoring is suddenly on every board packet.

Firefly Lattice was built for exactly this gap. AI-confirmed loiter detection on the parking-lot perimeter, gunshot acoustics inside, panic-button mesh on a private RF gateway that doesn't depend on your contracted guards being awake. Detect → Locate → Alert → Respond. The detection layer your incumbent contract doesn't cover.

15 minutes? I'm conscious of the active litigation; I'm not here to leverage it — just to make sure the technology gap is one OLOL can close.

— Ryan, Firefly

## Why this opening

- **Names both victims** — Truxillo (2019) and Jackson (2025). This is the hospital where the law is named after their own dead employee. Anything less specific is disrespectful.
- **Names Inner Parish Security by name** — that's the incumbent the lawsuit is naming. Mentioning it shows real research; mentioning it carefully (no leverage statement) shows judgment.
- **"loiter detection on the parking-lot perimeter"** — Pattie Jackson's killer loitered for hours. The product fit is exact, not analogous.
- **"detection layer your incumbent contract doesn't cover"** — frames Firefly as complementary, not replacement. Contract guards still have a role; Lattice fills the sensing layer.
- **Closing reassurance — "I'm not here to leverage it"** — a normal sales pitch would walk into the litigation as ammo. The right move at OLOL is the opposite. That stance is what gets the 15-minute meeting.
- **125 words. Two CCs. No exclamation marks.**

## Important constraints (per CLAUDE.md guardrails)

- This brief is for **internal Firefly use only**. Do not send the email until OLOL Legal posture is checked — the active wrongful-death litigation may make outbound contact about the Jackson incident inappropriate. The CEO's outside counsel may want to pre-clear any vendor outreach that touches the lawsuit.
- **Pattie Jackson and Lynne Truxillo are named in their honor**, not as marketing material. Any mention in the actual sent email must read with that tone or it should not be sent.
- Recommended escalation: route this brief to Firefly's CRO + Legal before any send.

## Open follow-ups (Tier-C)

- Confirm Inner Parish Security Corp's contract scope (is it just guards, or guards + sensor layer?)
- Pull FMOLHS IRS Form 990 — Schedule H — for WPV program narrative + budget line
- Identify named Director of Security at OLOL via IAHSS LA chapter
- Check whether OLOL post-Jackson has issued an RFP for security technology
- Note: AHA published a December 2024 case study on OLOL Health Louisiana ([aha.org link](https://www.aha.org/case-studies/2024-12-23-our-lady-lake-health-louisiana)) — pull for context on whatever program they were running pre-Jackson
